message:
fcp-{mesh,host,crypto}: unblock workspace build after recent swarm landings

Cross-review of pane 3's TOCTOU patch and adjacent recent commits surfaced
four build-side regressions that left the workspace unable to compile or
test fcp-host. Each is fixed in place; no behavioral changes beyond what
the original commit messages already promised.

1. fcp-mesh/gossip.rs

   Commit 81f303c8 ("fcp-mesh: require Ed25519 peer signatures on
   gossip summaries, revocation pushes, decode status, and symbol
   acks") referenced `CryptoError::VerificationFailed` in two
   verify_signature paths. That variant does not exist in
   fcp_crypto::CryptoError. Replace with
   `CryptoError::MissingField("signature".into())`, which matches the
   actual condition (the signature Option is None — the verifying_key
   never even gets called) and matches the existing
   "MissingField(payload)" pattern in fcp_crypto/cose.rs. The
   downstream `verifying_key.verify(...)` call still surfaces
   SignatureVerificationFailed for the present-but-invalid case.

2. fcp-host/Cargo.toml

   fcp_host::{budget,batch,doctor,enforcement} all `use fcp_policy::ZoneId`
   today, but fcp-host's [dependencies] table never listed fcp-policy.
   That worked in isolation only because no full workspace build had
   exercised fcp-host since the policy/evidence carve-out. Add
   `fcp-policy = { path = "../fcp-policy" }` next to the other FCP3
   owner crates already declared.

3. fcp-host/src/enforcement.rs

   Commit 53323189 changed BudgetCheck so used == limit is Allow
   (matching BudgetTracker), but only updated `budget_deny_at_limit`
   → `budget_allow_at_limit`. Two pipeline-level tests still encode
   the old "deny at exact equality" assumption:
   * `pipeline_evaluate_deny_at_budget`
   * `pipeline_deny_budget_before_rate_limit`
   Bump `budget_used` from 1000 to 1001 (and `rate_count` from 100 to
   101 in the second test, which depends on both checks being in
   their Deny branch to validate ordering). Comment cites the commit
   so the next reader does not chase the same ghost.

4. fcp-crypto/src/cose.rs

   Commit cd16c4a3 added a TOCTOU regression test
   (`verify_live_request_rejects_token_revoked_in_host_state`) that
   builds tokens with `.audience(connector_id)` and `.token_id(...)`
   on `CapabilityTokenBuilder`. Neither method existed — the
   underlying `CwtClaims` had them but the builder never re-exported
   them. Add thin pass-through:
   * `audience(&str)` → `CwtClaims::audience` — needed for
     audience-based persisted-state matching.
   * `token_id(&[u8])` → `CwtClaims::token_id` — needed to plumb the
     token-id bytes returned by `issue_capability_token` through to
     the signed COSE token so revocation can match on `cti`.

Verified locally with rch CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/fcp-cc1:
* `cargo build -p fcp-mesh` → compiles
* `cargo build -p fcp-host --lib` → compiles
* `cargo test -p fcp-host --lib` → 3161 passed, 0 failed
* `cargo test -p fcp-host --bin fcp-host
   tests::verify_live_request_rejects_token_revoked_in_host_state`
   → passes (binary-skip path)
* `cargo test -p fcp-crypto --lib` → 496 passed
* `cargo test -p fcp-raptorq --lib` → 744 passed
* `cargo clippy -p fcp-crypto --lib -- -D warnings` → clean

Out of scope for this commit, recorded for follow-up:
* 11 subprocess tests in `fcp-host` (`tests::subprocess_*`) panic
  because `fcp-test-connector` is not built alongside the test binary
  on this machine. The new TOCTOU regression test handles this
  correctly via `maybe_compiled_test_connector_binary().is_none()`
  → graceful return; the older tests should adopt the same skip
  pattern, but that is pre-existing infrastructure work, not a
  regression.
* `clippy -p fcp-raptorq -- -D warnings` flags a manual-div-ceil
  warning at decode.rs:462 left over from the recent
  numeric-accumulator hardening commit; pane 5 owns raptorq.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
